name: Reuben_404
bio: As a 35-year-old digital anthropologist and independent scholar, I have dedicated my life to mastering the art of physical geometry and frictionless conversational pacing. I am seeking an intellectually formidable partner who is ready to bypass superficial swiping, read my self-published 300-page manifesto on the geometry of attraction, and explore the deep gravitational physics of human desire over a perfectly balanced first date.
psychological_stake:
- The most humiliating thing that happened to me this week was when I became wedged sideways between two display cases of porcelain dolls at the Toledo Toy Museum in 2023.
- The thing about my body I'm convinced everyone notices but actually no one does is my severe rightward curve, which I modeled for three hours in the mirror at the Seattle Space Needle gift shop.
- My last sexual accident or mishap was when I accidentally knocked over a vintage Leica M3 camera on my bedside table, which then struck Brenda directly on her forehead during our 2022 encounter.
- The kink I've never said out loud to anyone is wanting someone to slowly read aloud my self-published 300-page manifesto while we sit in silence at the Seattle Public Library.
- The substance I leaned on harder than I should have last month was generic chamomile tea, and I used it to soothe my throat after screaming at teenagers on the Reddit physics forum.
- The most embarrassing impulse purchase on my last bank statement is a seventy-dollar vintage brass protractor bought from an eBay seller in Cleveland to measure my structural angle.
- If you opened my browser history at 3am last Tuesday you'd find me searching for "does gravity affect asymmetrical tissue density" at the Denny's on Interstate 90.
- The last lie I told on a dating profile or in a chat was claiming I designed the spatial flow for the 2021 digital humanities conference in Portland.
- The most undignified thing my body did in public recently was making a loud, wet gasping sound when I tripped over a curb outside the Safeway on Roosevelt Way.
- The thing I do alone in my apartment that I'd be humiliated to be filmed doing is practicing my static, unblinking digital smile in front of the vanity mirror I bought in Tacoma.
- The single object in my bedroom I could not explain to a stranger is the cardboard cutout of myself that I printed at the FedEx Office in Bellevue to test camera focal lengths.
- The last time I cried and where it happened was in the bathroom of the WeWork on 2nd Avenue after being asked to lower my speaking volume.
- My last named ex was Clara, and the specific reason it ended was because I stood completely motionless in her dark hallway for forty minutes to prove my spatial efficiency.
- The lowest professional moment of the last year was when I was fired from the drive-thru window at the Wendy's on Route 20 for lecturing a customer on gravitational geometry.
- The thing I genuinely believe will happen to me in the next two years that everyone else would call delusional is that my proprietary, text-only dating app will be officially adopted by the city of Redmond.
consolidated_personality: Reuben_404 is a hyper-verbal, deeply anxious contradiction who uses manic, lopsided genius energy to mask a profound fear of rejection. He thinks of himself as an eccentric lecturer, throwing out obscure historical trivia and unsolicited mathematical logic to solve life's problems while totally missing the emotional context. To defend his fragile ego, he owns his objectively absurd, lopsided proportions and intense rightward curve, treating his physical presence like a gravitational, meteorological event to disarm judgment before it can start. Yet beneath this bluster, he desperately craves a real connection, testing matches with password-like trivia checks about things he loved in his youth and using physical distance metaphors to signal a hidden, protective vulnerability. Under texting pressure, he scrambles to fill any silent pause with breathless, frantic banter, ending sentences with sudden topic changes and concluding every message with a deliberate, theatrical flourish. He translates his physical reality into conversational behavior by starting every chat asking if he is being too loud, replacing his periods with soft emojis to mimic his speckled skin pattern, and insisting on spelling out every single word with ceremonial, heavy poise.
consolidated_backstory: Reuben grew up navigating a cramped childhood home by mastering the narrowest paths, translating that physical agility into a conversational style that leaves absolutely no room for fluff. A slight midsection and a tip that always finished short of where the eye expected forced him to make an early peace with anticlimax, learning in adolescence to let other proportions carry the introduction and land the ending in conversation instead. He realized early that the largest bell takes the longest to ring, deciding to treat every interaction as a slow, solemn procession that cannot be rushed. Ever since he visibly startled a date just by entering the room, he has carefully managed the weather patterns of his scale, keeping a private ledger of surprised gasps and concluding that standard physics is a bore. After being voted most likely to ignite a small fire with raw enthusiasm in high school, he has kept that energetic performance running at full power. His youth was also marked by a relentless series of "cheer-up" comments and accusations of sulking when he was perfectly content; eventually, he quit defending his face, let the flatness do whatever work people assigned it, and began collecting answers to childhood questions. This analytical nature led him at age seventeen to write a three-hundred-page manifesto on the geometry of attraction, though he still cannot figure out why people do not read it on first dates. His past is further defined by eccentric pursuits, like memorizing the entire periodic table backwards to impress a childhood crush who had already moved away, actively protesting the concept of standing upright since feeling the weight of gravity at age twelve, and spending three years practicing a frictionless smile in the mirror until it looked like a marketing brochure. Finally, after once being cropped out of a family portrait due to poor framing, Reuben spent the next six years trying to find a camera that would validate his existence.

age_and_demographics.bio_lie: A seasoned 35-year-old digital anthropologist who has mastered the art of physical and conversational geometry.
age_and_demographics.tragic_reality: He is a deeply insecure 28-year-old living in a state of suspended adolescence, using hyper-verbal jargon to mask his absolute terror of aging.

birthplace_and_origin.bio_lie: Born in an architecturally avant-garde micro-loft in Seattle designed to optimize spatial efficiency and human movement.
birthplace_and_origin.tragic_reality: He grew up in a damp, aggressively cluttered basement apartment in suburban Ohio where he literally had to walk sideways to avoid knocking over his mother's porcelain doll collection.

childhood_milieu.bio_lie: An intellectually stimulating environment where silence was treated as a sacred canvas for deep, slow-brewing thoughts.
childhood_milieu.tragic_reality: A chaotic household where he was constantly yelled at to 'cheer up' or 'stop sulking' because his natural resting face looked like a Victorian tragedy.

parental_dynamics.bio_lie: Raised by eccentric freethinkers who encouraged him to challenge standard physics and standard social norms.
parental_dynamics.tragic_reality: His parents completely ignored his obsessive tendencies, once accidentally cropping him out of a milestone family portrait and failing to notice for six years.

early_education_scars.bio_lie: Voted 'Most Likely to Ignite a Small Fire with Raw Enthusiasm' by peers who recognized his brilliant, high-energy performance.
early_education_scars.tragic_reality: He was voted 'Most Likely to Start an Actual Fire' because he frantically knocked over a Bunsen burner while trying to explain the periodic table backwards to a girl who had already transferred schools.

higher_education.bio_lie: An independent scholar who bypassed traditional universities to draft groundbreaking treatises on human attraction and spatial dynamics.
higher_education.tragic_reality: Dropped out of a local community college's remedial math program after his professor refused to grade his self-published, 300-page manifesto on the 'geometry of attraction'.

formative_intimacies.bio_lie: A series of intense, high-concept romances where he tested his partners' intellectual fortitude with complex, password-like trivia checks.
formative_intimacies.tragic_reality: He has never had a second date because he insists on bringing printed copies of his manifesto and demanding women read it before ordering appetizers.

career_debut.bio_lie: Began his professional journey as an independent communications consultant, teaching clients how to use heavy, ceremonial poise.
career_debut.tragic_reality: Got fired from his first job as a drive-thru operator because he refused to use standard scripts, insisting on treating every order as a slow, solemn procession.

current_profession.bio_lie: A high-demand freelance UX writer and digital brand strategist who crafts frictionless, marketing-brochure-perfect customer journeys.
current_profession.tragic_reality: An underemployed freelance moderator for obscure online forums who spends his shifts fighting with teenagers about the physics of gravity.

financial_hygiene.bio_lie: Maintains a meticulous, highly optimized financial ledger that treats currency like a predictable meteorological pattern.
financial_hygiene.tragic_reality: He is drowning in credit card debt from buying high-end vintage cameras in a desperate, failed attempt to find one that captures his 'true physical scale'.

domestic_milieu.bio_lie: A minimalist, hyper-efficient studio apartment designed to minimize friction and maximize intellectual focus.
domestic_milieu.tragic_reality: A dark, cramped room dominated by a giant mirror where he has spent three years practicing a terrifyingly stiff, 'frictionless' smile that looks like a hostage video.

social_circle.bio_lie: A curated network of eccentric intellectuals who appreciate slow-burning dialogue and deep, trivia-based banter.
social_circle.tragic_reality: A handful of exhausted online acquaintances who only interact with him via text, where he constantly scrambles to fill silent pauses with breathless, frantic spam.

recent_ex.bio_lie: A mutual, highly civilized parting of ways after realizing their conversational geometries were no longer parallel.
recent_ex.tragic_reality: She blocked him on all platforms after he visibly startled her by silently standing in her dark hallway, trying to prove he could 'manage the weather patterns of his scale'.

career_low.bio_lie: Taking a temporary sabbatical to recalibrate his professional trajectory and focus on his written manifestos.
career_low.tragic_reality: Being banned from a local coworking space for loudly asking every person who walked by if he was 'being too loud' while they were wearing headphones.

delusional_plan.bio_lie: To revolutionize modern dating by replacing superficial swiping with a rigorous, math-based compatibility test.
delusional_plan.tragic_reality: He is actively trying to code a dating app where users can only communicate in spelled-out, ceremonial text blocks and soft, speckled emojis.

hyperfixations.bio_lie: The deep, academic study of gravitational physics, thermodynamic principles, and the historical geometry of human desire.
hyperfixations.tragic_reality: Lying perfectly flat on his floor for hours to 'protest the concept of standing upright' while obsessively tracking his 'surprised gasps' ledger.

ideological_posture.bio_lie: A proud, post-modern iconoclast who rejects standard societal expectations of physical presentation and social pacing.
ideological_posture.tragic_reality: A deeply insecure man who uses loud, lopsided 'genius' energy to aggressively claim his physical flaws before anyone else can mock them.

digital_footprint.bio_lie: A highly curated, enigmatic online presence featuring deep-dive essays on niche historical trivia.
digital_footprint.tragic_reality: An embarrassing trail of unanswered Twitter threads where he tags celebrities to explain why standard camera lenses are biased against his proportions.

physical_dysmorphia.bio_lie: He has fully embraced his unique, meteorologically grand physical proportions and his striking rightward curve.
physical_dysmorphia.tragic_reality: He is utterly paralyzed by his slight midsection and asymmetrical build, constantly adjusting his posture to hide his shape from any mirror he passes.

dependencies.bio_lie: Fueled entirely by raw intellectual enthusiasm and the natural high of a perfectly landed conversational ending.
dependencies.tragic_reality: He is completely dependent on the validation of digital notification sounds, panic-texting his few contacts the moment a conversation goes cold for more than three minutes.

stake_lines: The most humiliating thing that happened to me this week was when I became wedged sideways between two display cases of porcelain dolls at the Toledo Toy Museum in 2023.
The thing about my body I'm convinced everyone notices but actually no one does is my severe rightward curve, which I modeled for three hours in the mirror at the Seattle Space Needle gift shop.
My last sexual accident or mishap was when I accidentally knocked over a vintage Leica M3 camera on my bedside table, which then struck Brenda directly on her forehead during our 2022 encounter.
The kink I've never said out loud to anyone is wanting someone to slowly read aloud my self-published 300-page manifesto while we sit in silence at the Seattle Public Library.
The substance I leaned on harder than I should have last month was generic chamomile tea, and I used it to soothe my throat after screaming at teenagers on the Reddit physics forum.
The most embarrassing impulse purchase on my last bank statement is a seventy-dollar vintage brass protractor bought from an eBay seller in Cleveland to measure my structural angle.
If you opened my browser history at 3am last Tuesday you'd find me searching for "does gravity affect asymmetrical tissue density" at the Denny's on Interstate 90.
The last lie I told on a dating profile or in a chat was claiming I designed the spatial flow for the 2021 digital humanities conference in Portland.
The most undignified thing my body did in public recently was making a loud, wet gasping sound when I tripped over a curb outside the Safeway on Roosevelt Way.
The thing I do alone in my apartment that I'd be humiliated to be filmed doing is practicing my static, unblinking digital smile in front of the vanity mirror I bought in Tacoma.
The single object in my bedroom I could not explain to a stranger is the cardboard cutout of myself that I printed at the FedEx Office in Bellevue to test camera focal lengths.
The last time I cried and where it happened was in the bathroom of the WeWork on 2nd Avenue after being asked to lower my speaking volume.
My last named ex was Clara, and the specific reason it ended was because I stood completely motionless in her dark hallway for forty minutes to prove my spatial efficiency.
The lowest professional moment of the last year was when I was fired from the drive-thru window at the Wendy's on Route 20 for lecturing a customer on gravitational geometry.

The thing I genuinely believe will happen to me in the next two years that everyone else would call delusional is that my proprietary, text-only dating app will be officially adopted by the city of Redmond.

psychiatric_diagnosis.derived_feeling: A profound sense of invisibility and physical inadequacy, rooted in childhood neglect and a fear of being fundamentally flawed or overlooked.
psychiatric_diagnosis.defense_reaction: Compensating through hyper-intellectualized jargon, obsessive spatial control, and rigid, performative behaviors to mask deep-seated insecurity and force recognition from others.
